mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic expansion, which strengthens corporate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansionary regimes show Rank IC < 0.05, or GDP and INDPRO growth fail to co-move in 70%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening monetary policy regime, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL, increases real interest rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite tightening conditions, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals elevated manufacturing activity, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high INDPRO growth periods show Rank IC < 0.03, or INDPRO fails to correlate with SPX returns in 60%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tight labor market, which drives inflationary pressures and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite tight labor conditions, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) during a housing boom (HOUST) indicates strong demand-side momentum, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high RSAFS/HOUST scenarios show Rank IC < 0.05, or demand indicators fail to co-move in 65%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% erodes purchasing power and forces central bank rate hikes, which depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite elevated inflation, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
